records.title: ADR-IMP-020 through ADR-IMP-022 identifier and title reuse
records.description: Implementation Planning Section 4 used ADR-IMP-020 through ADR-IMP-022 as deferred placeholder identifiers for later secret-provider, remote-configuration, and configuration-write-back decisions. The final Section 10 ADR disposition used the same identifiers for testing, observability, and reliability decisions.
records.potential_impact: Without lifecycle-aware normalization, the publication inventory would attach the wrong titles and bodies to accepted ADR identifiers.
records.resolution: The exact final Section 10 disposition plus explicit user approval controls the locked ADR corpus. The Section 4 names are retained only as superseded pre-lock placeholders and do not define the accepted ADRs.

records.title: Pre-lock ADR numbering plan differs from final accepted dispositions
records.description: Implementation Planning Sections 1 through 4 used several ADR identifiers for proposed or deferred subjects that were later reassigned or refined by the final approved section dispositions, including identifiers in the ADR-IMP-005 through ADR-IMP-019 and ADR-IMP-023 range.
records.potential_impact: A publication based on early planning lists could attach obsolete subjects to accepted identifiers.
records.resolution: Treat early names as pre-lock planning history. The exact later section disposition that explicitly accepts each ADR controls its final title and body. No approved architecture is reopened.

records.title: G1 roadmap numbering differs from executed readiness-review numbering
records.description: The recovered thirteen-area G1 roadmap places First-Domain Viability and Launch Recommendation at Section 13, while later execution and approval evidence identifies the formal First-Domain Viability & Implementation Readiness Review as G1 Section 7.
records.potential_impact: Without lifecycle-aware normalization, publication history could present the planning roadmap as the final executed section sequence or merge the readiness disposition into the wrong source family.
records.resolution: Retain the thirteen-area artifact as roadmap and coverage history. Treat the later Section 7 evidence as controlling for the executed review and approval event, while preserving the review as its own publication source family. The architectural outcomes are compatible and no decision is reopened.
